---
title: Quarto with JamsEDU
description: Crash course for using Quarto files in a JamsEDU site, including include shortcodes and JamsEDU built-in feature markup patterns.
---

# Quarto with JamsEDU

JamsEDU lets you drop standard Quarto `.qmd` files into your project and have them rendered alongside your `.jhp` pages with the same site chrome. No extra build step on your part: JamsEDU calls Quarto internally, captures the HTML output, and wraps it in your template.

Use this page as your quick-start guide for authoring `.qmd` files in a JamsEDU project. See the [companion demo](./quarto-demo.html) for a live example of the finished output.

## How It Works

1. You create content in `*.qmd` files.
2. JamsEDU calls Quarto to render each file. Quarto resolves its own features (e.g. `{{< include >}}`, fenced `:::` blocks) and produces an HTML fragment.
3. JamsEDU injects that fragment into your `quarto.template` layout (`quarto` in `.jamsedu/config.js`), so the published page ships with the same header, footer, and styling as your `.jhp` pages.

By design, JamsEDU ignores any Quarto website-level configuration (navigation, search, listings, etc.) in your `.qmd` files and applies its own safe defaults.

## Configuration

Quarto options live under the `quarto` key in `.jamsedu/config.js` and should be an object with the following properties:

```js
quarto: {
    /**
     * The .jhp file used as the wrapper template for all .qmd pages.
     * A default template ships with a fresh JamsEDU install.
     */
    template: 'src/templates/quarto.jhp',

    /**
     * Output directory where Quarto-generated assets (images, figures, etc.) are placed.
     */
    assetsDir: 'quarto-assets',

    /**
     * Staging directory for temporary build files. Should be git-ignored.
     */
    workingDir: '.quarto'
}
```

## The Template

The default template that ships with JamsEDU is minimal:

```html

    $include('/header.html');
    $echo(quartoHtml);
    $include('/footer.html');

```

If you modify the template or create your own, you must include `$echo(quartoHtml);` somewhere in it. That call is where the rendered `.qmd` content gets placed.

## Quarto Project YAML

You can provide your own Quarto project YAML to set global `.qmd` settings and metadata. Place one of the following files inside `.jamsedu/` (only the first one found is used, checked in this order):

1. `quarto.yml`
2. `_quarto.yml`
3. `quarto-project.yml`

Note that some keys are managed internally by JamsEDU and cannot be overridden.

## Using JamsEDU Features in `.qmd` Files

#### Mermaid Diagrams

Quarto's built-in Mermaid syntax will work in JamsEDU sites, but for best results use the JamsEDU fenced blocks instead. To add [Mermaid diagrams](./mermaid-diagrams.html) in `.qmd` files, use either of the following:

```markdown
::: {.jams-mermaid}
flowchart LR
  A --> B --> C
:::
```

```markdown
::: {.jams-diagram}
sequenceDiagram
  A->>B: hello
:::
```

#### KaTeX math

To write math equations in `.qmd` files, use the standard `$…$` for inline and `$$…$$` for display (block) math.

Here is an example of `$...$` (inline) $E = mc^2$ math, followed by `$$...$` (block) math:

$$
\int_{0}^{1} x^2\,dx = \frac{1}{3}
$$

If a formula misbehaves (money-like text, complex TeX, edge cases), you can use an explicit fenced block or inline shortcode instead.

**Fenced blocks:**

```markdown
::: {.jams-katex .macro}
\f = #1f(#2)
:::
```

```markdown
::: {.jams-math}
\f\relax{x} = \int_{-\infty}^\infty \f\hat\xi\,e^{2 \pi i \xi x} \,d\xi
:::
```

```{=html}
<div class="callout info icon">
    <p>
        Fenced math blocks can declare macro functions available to the entire page. This is a feature specific to JamsEDU, see the <a href="./katex-math.html">KaTeX guide</a> for more details.
    </p>
</div>
```

**Inline shortcode:**

For explicit inline math without `$` delimiters, use the `{{{< jams-math-inline >}}}` shortcode (aliased as `jams-katex-inline`). You can add `macro="true"` for macro definitions. See the [KaTeX guide](./katex-math.html) for more details.

```{shortcodes=false}
The sum {{< jams-math-inline formula="\alpha+\beta=\gamma" >}} appears in line.
```

#### Embedded PDF

To [embed a PDF](./embedded-pdf.html) in `.qmd` files, use the following fenced block that contains only the URL to the PDF:

```markdown
::: {.jams-pdf}
/assets/docs/jamsedu-demo.pdf
:::
```

#### Responsive video

To auto embed a [responsive video](./built-in-features.html#responsive-video) in `.qmd` files, use the following fenced blocks based on your needs:

```markdown
::: {.jams-video}
https://youtu.be/NpEaa2P7qZI
:::
```

```markdown
::: {.jams-video cite="Sample video citation"}
https://youtu.be/NpEaa2P7qZI
:::
```

```{=html}
<div class="callout warning icon">
    <p>
        JamsEDU only supports the following video hosting platforms: YouTube, Vimeo, Dailymotion, Rumble, and Twitch.
    </p>
</div>
```

If you need to embed an unsupported video format you should use a standard HTML code block:

````markdown
```{=html}
<iframe src="..." title="Embedded video" ...></iframe>
```
````

#### Rich text

To add a [rich text editor](./rich-text.html) in `.qmd` files, use a fenced block with the `{.jams-rich}` class. The `placeholder` attribute is optional. To prepopulate the editor with content, include it in a `{=html}` block:

````markdown
::: {.jams-rich placeholder="Answer in a few sentences."}
```{=html}
<p>Draft answer goes here.</p>
```
:::
````

```{=html}
<div class="callout warning icon">
    <p>
        Only basic HTML is supposed in rich text editors, any tags not in this list will break your editor: `p`, `a`, `strong`, `em`, `u`, `ul`, `ol`, or `h1`
    </p>
</div>
```

#### Tiny Document

To add a [Tiny Document](./tiny-document.html) in `.qmd` files, use a fenced block with the `{.jams-document}` class. The `{=html}` block is required and must contain valid Tiny Document HTML and classes:

````markdown
::: {.jams-document}
```{=html}
<div class="title">Worksheet</div>
<p class="instructions">Try the prompts below.</p>
<!-- The rest of the document here... -->
```
:::
````

#### Code highlighting

JamsEDU already supports [code highlighting](./highlight-js-lite.html) so code blocks (```` ``` ````) will automatically be highlighted with JamsEDU's version of Highlight.js:

````markdown
```javascript
const sum = (a, b) => {
    return a + b;
};

console.log(sum(10, 32));
```
````

## Migration tips

- Put `.qmd` sources under **`srcDir`** from your JamsEdu configuration.
- Point **`quarto.template`** at a single JHP file for shared Quarto HTML (header, footer, scripts, styles).
- Place any extra files (images, CSS, scripts, etc.) from your Quarto project into your JamsEDU `assetsDir` so they stay available at their usual URLs.

## Sample `.qmd` page

To see all of these examples running on a live page, visit the [companion `.qmd` demo](./quarto-demo.html).
